DEMURRER to ANSWER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-12-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances via Zoom Conference
MICHAEL P. ROONEY, ESQ., Michael Rooney Law Office, P.C. (415) 533-0282 mike@mikerooneylaw.com for PLAINTIFF MICHAEL VIGIL
Lisa Barnett Sween, Esq., JACKSON LEWIS P.C. (415) 394-9400 Lisa.Sween@jacksonlewis.com
Attorneys for Defendant CLOUDFLARE, INC.

Matter is not contested. Plaintiff seeks clarification of the court's tentative ruling. Discussion held, not argued.

After discussion, the Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF MICHAEL VIGIL's DEMURRER to ANSWER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT

"Plaintiff's demurrer to defendant's verified answer" is adjudged as follows.

The answer laundry-lists 29 affirmative defenses. To survive demurrer, an affirmative defense must be "averred as carefully and with as much detail as the facts which constitute the cause of action and are alleged in the complaint." (FPI Development, Inc. v. Nakashima (1991) 231 Cal.App.3d 367, 384; see also Edmon & Karnow, California Practice Guide: Civil Procedure Before Trial (TRG 2016) 6:459.) Plaintiff says affirmative defenses 2-5, 8-9, 11-12, 14-15 and 23-28 do not meet this standard. (Memo. 4:7-10.) The court agrees and sustains this demurrer with leave to amend.

The other demurrers assert inconsequential points - some bordering on the frivolous - so they are overruled.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)
